title: A failed war experiment made this man $140 million #Shorts
desc:
Silly Putty started as a World War Two failure: chemists chasing a synthetic rubber substitute mixed boric acid with silicone oil and got a bouncing goo that couldn't replace a single tire. In 1949 a marketing man named Peter Hodgson, already $12,000 in debt, borrowed $147, packed the goo into plastic eggs, and sold 250,000 of them in three days after one New Yorker mention. It later orbited the moon on Apollo 8, and when Hodgson died in 1976 his estate was worth about $140 million.

#BrokeToBuilt #history #invention #SillyPutty #shorts
